name: A-180-2
description: Passive multiple. Eight interconnected jacks shipped from factory as two independent 4-way multiples (jacks 1–4 and 5–8). Closing an internal solder bridge joins all eight into a single 8-way mult. No active buffering.

inputs.name: Jack 1
inputs.description: Any of jacks 1–4 can act as input; the other three in the group carry the same signal.

inputs.name: Jack 5
inputs.description: Any of jacks 5–8 can act as input for the second group (factory default) or is joined to jacks 1–4 if the solder bridge is closed.

outputs.name: Jack 2
outputs.description: Passive copy of whichever jack in group 1 carries the source signal.

outputs.name: Jack 6
outputs.description: Passive copy within group 2 (or extended group 1 if bridge closed).

outputs.name: Jack 7
outputs.description: Passive copy within group 2 (or extended group 1 if bridge closed).

outputs.name: Jack 8
outputs.description: Passive copy within group 2 (or extended group 1 if bridge closed).

behaviors.name: Dual 1-to-3 (factory default)
behaviors.description: Solder bridge open: two isolated groups of four inter-connected jacks. Patch one source into any jack of a group, take three copies from the remaining three jacks of that group.

behaviors.name: Single 1-to-7
behaviors.description: Closing the PCB bridge joins both groups into a single bus of eight interconnected jacks — one source, up to seven copies.

behaviors.name: Passive = no buffer
behaviors.trigger: distributing pitch CV to multiple VCOs
behaviors.description: Each additional VCO added to a passive mult loads the source and can drop pitch CV slightly. Doepfer explicitly recommends the buffered A-185-2 for 1V/oct distribution to avoid tuning drift. Safe for audio, gates, clocks, and non-critical CV.

behaviors.name: Bidirectional summing caveat
behaviors.trigger: cables from multiple sources plugged into one group
behaviors.description: All jacks in a group are hard-wired together, so plugging two sources simultaneously ties those outputs together — not a mixer. The group then presents a short or contention depending on output impedances.

patchIdeas.name: Clock distribution
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock → Jack 1 · Jack 2/3/4 → sequencer, drum, LFO sync
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-180-2:
- Master Clock (Clk) c> A-180-2 (Jack 1)
- A-180-2 (Jack 2) c> Sequencer (Clk)
- A-180-2 (Jack 3) c> Drum (Trig)
- A-180-2 (Jack 4) c> LFO (Sync)

patchIdeas.name: Gate to many envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: Keyboard gate → Jack 1 · drives four ADSRs in parallel for stacked modulation
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-180-2:
- Keyboard (Gate) g> A-180-2 (Jack 1)
- A-180-2 (Jack 2) g> ADSR 1 (Gate)
- A-180-2 (Jack 3) g> ADSR 2 (Gate)
- A-180-2 (Jack 4) g> ADSR 3 (Gate)

patchIdeas.name: Two independent splits
patchIdeas.patch: Gate in group 1 · audio in group 2 · two unrelated distributions in 2 HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-180-2:
- Gate Source (Gate) g> A-180-2 (Jack 1)
- A-180-2 (Jack 2) g> ADSR (Gate)
- A-180-2 (Jack 3) g> VCA (CV)
- Audio (Audio) -> A-180-2 (Jack 5)
- A-180-2 (Jack 6) -> Delay (In)
- A-180-2 (Jack 7) -> Reverb (In)

patchIdeas.name: 8-way mult (bridge closed)
patchIdeas.patch: One LFO fans out to seven destinations after user closes the solder bridge
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-180-2:
* solder bridge: closed (1-to-7 mode)
- LFO (Out) >> A-180-2 (Jack 1)
- A-180-2 (Jack 2) >> VCF (CV)
- A-180-2 (Jack 3) >> VCA (CV)
- A-180-2 (Jack 4) >> VCO (PWM)
- A-180-2 (Jack 5) >> FX (CV)
- A-180-2 (Jack 6) >> Reverb (CV)
